journal homepage: www.elsevier.com/locate/molstr
Synthesis, structural characterization and antibacterial activity
evaluation of novel quinolone-1,2,3-triazole-benzimidazole hybrids
Khadija El Gadali a , b, Meriem Rafya a, Az-eddine El Mansouri c, Mohamed Maatallah b,
Arie Van-der lee d, Ahmad Mehdi e, Abdelaaziz Ouahrouch a, Fatiha Benkhalti a,
Yogesh S. Sanghvi f, Moha Taourirte a , ∗, Hassan B. Lazrek b, ∗
a Laboratoire de Recherche en développement durable et santé, Faculty of Sciences and Technology Gueliz (FSTG), BP549, Marrakech 40 0 0 0, Morocco
b Laboratory of molecular Chemistry, Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Sciences Semlalia, Marrakech 40 0 0 0, Morocco
c University of the free state Faculty of natural and agricultural sciences Chemistry department 205 Nelson Mandela, Bloemfontein, 9301, Republic of South
Africa
d Institut Européen des Membranes, IEM, UMR 5635, Univ. Montpellier, CNRS, ENSCM, 34095 Montpellier, France
e ICGM, UMR5253 1919, Route de Mende 34293 Montpellier cedex 5, France
f Rasayan Inc. 2802 Crystal Ridge Road, Encinitas, CA 92024-6615, USA
a r t i c l e i n f o a b s t r a c t
Article history: In this investigation, a series of 2-quinolone-benzimidazole hybrids linked by a 1,2,3-triazole moiety were
Received 19 December 2022 prepared using copper-catalyzed Huisgen 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition and condensation reaction. The newly
Revised 4 February 2023 synthesized compounds were characterized by 1 H NMR, 13 C NMR, single crystal XRD and HRMS analysis.
Accepted 14 February 2023 The density functional theory (DFT) calculations were performed using B3LYP/6–31 + G (d,p) basis level
Available online 16 February 2023
set for the optimization of molecular geometries of compounds 7a, 7c, 7f and 7l The conformational
Keywords: analysis predicted the existence of two main conformations: a closed and an open conformation. In vitro
2-quinolone antibacterial activity was assessed against Gram-positive bacterium Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus) and
Benzimidazole Gram-negative bacterium Escherichia coli (E. coli) . The bioassays revealed that compounds 7a, 7c, 7f and 7l
1,2,3-triazole inhibited the growth of E. coli at a concentration of 0.14 μmol/mL, which was nearly 2-fold stronger than
Antibacterial activity the control drug ampicillin (0.26 μmol/mL). Besides, compounds 7f and 7l exhibited the most promising
Molecular docking
activity against S. aureus with MIC and MBC values of 0.07 μmol/mL for 7f and 0.14 μmol/mL for 7l com-
DFT studies
pared to 0.27 μmol/mL for ampicillin. These results were supported by the chemical reactivity parameters
and electrostatic surface potential (MEP) obtained using theoretical calculations. Molecular docking study
of the most active compounds using DNA gyrase A and DNA gyrase B proteins of S. aureus also confirmed
the experimental findings.
©2023 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.
1. Introduction severe secondary bacterial pneumonia following influenza A virus
(IAV) infection, which results in significant morbidity and mortal-
According to the WHO, antimicrobial resistance (AMR) is a ity [3] . More importantly, it has been found that S. aureus triggers
growing threat to human health globally. This phenomenon occurs respiratory complications in patients hospitalized with COVID-19
when germs like bacteria and fungi develop mutations that make and is associated with high case fatality rate [4] . Based on these
antibiotics ineffective [1] . Among these germs is S. aureus, which facts, there is a continuous necessity for the search of new effec-
has the ability to develop resistance to many antimicrobial agents. tive antimicrobial drugs to overcome bacterial resistance. The 2-
It can induce various clinical diseases, including mild skin and soft quinolone nucleus is one of the most useful frameworks in the
tissue infections, bacteremia, infective endocarditis and osteomyeli- synthesis and development of impending antibiotics. 2-Quinolones
tis [2] . Additionally, S. aureus is the primary causative strain of and their analogues are important nitrogen-containing heterocy-
cles, widely present in nature as alkaloids (such as Viridicatol or
Viridicatin ( Fig. 1 )) [5–8] and possess an extensive spectrum of bi-
ological properties among which is the antimicrobial [ 9 , 10 ]. Many
∗ Corresponding authors.
E-mail addresses: m.taourirte@uca.ma (M. Taourirte), hblazrek50@gmail.com ,
of the quinolone-based scaffolds with antibacterial activity, were
hblazrek@uca.ac.ma (H.B. Lazrek) . found to act mainly by inhibiting the activity of bacteria type II
https://doi.org/10.1016/j.molstruc.2023.135179

Article history: In this investigation, a series of 2-quinolone-benzimidazole hybrids linked by a 1,2,3-triazole moiety were
Received 19 December 2022 prepared using copper-catalyzed Huisgen 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition and condensation reaction. The newly
Revised 4 February 2023 synthesized compounds were characterized by 1 H NMR, 13 C NMR, single crystal XRD and HRMS analysis.
Accepted 14 February 2023 The density functional theory (DFT) calculations were performed using B3LYP/6–31 + G (d,p) basis level
Available online 16 February 2023
set for the optimization of molecular geometries of compounds 7a, 7c, 7f and 7l The conformational
Keywords: analysis predicted the existence of two main conformations: a closed and an open conformation. In vitro
2-quinolone antibacterial activity was assessed against Gram-positive bacterium Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus) and
Benzimidazole Gram-negative bacterium Escherichia coli (E. coli) . The bioassays revealed that compounds 7a, 7c, 7f and 7l
1,2,3-triazole inhibited the growth of E. coli at a concentration of 0.14 μmol/mL, which was nearly 2-fold stronger than
Antibacterial activity the control drug ampicillin (0.26 μmol/mL). Besides, compounds 7f and 7l exhibited the most promising
Molecular docking
activity against S. aureus with MIC and MBC values of 0.07 μmol/mL for 7f and 0.14 μmol/mL for 7l com-
DFT studies
pared to 0.27 μmol/mL for ampicillin. These results were supported by the chemical reactivity parameters
and electrostatic surface potential (MEP) obtained using theoretical calculations. Molecular docking study
of the most active compounds using DNA gyrase A and DNA gyrase B proteins of S. aureus also confirmed
the experimental findings.
